description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages, build and test, using Docker images wireshark/wireshark-ubuntu-dev for Ubuntu builds (including a clang-10 variant), debian:stable for Debian builds and tests, centos:7 for CentOS RPM builds and tests, fedora for Fedora RPM builds and tests, and opensuse/leap:15.1 for openSUSE RPM builds and tests. In the Ubuntu build jobs (.build-ubuntu and clang-10) run a before_script that updates apt, runs ./tools/debian-setup.sh with optional and test dependencies, creates a non‑root user, sets locale and PYTEST_ADDOPTS, makes a build directory and cd into it, then execute cmake -GNinja $CMAKE_ARGS .., ninja, ninja test-programs, chown the files, optionally setcap on run/dumpcap, and finally run pytest-3 as the created user; clang-10 also sets CC=clang-10 and CXX=clang++-10. The Debian build job runs ./tools/debian-setup.sh with install‑deb‑deps, then dpkg-buildpackage -b --no-sign -jauto, creates a debian-packages directory and moves the generated .deb files there, publishing them as artifacts for 3 days. Each RPM build job (centos‑7, fedora, opensuse‑15.1) installs required system packages (yum, dnf, or zypper), runs tools/rpm-setup.sh with optional flags, creates a build directory, runs cmake3 -GNinja .. (or cmake -GNinja .. on openSUSE), then ninja-build rpm-package (or ninja rpm-package), and stores the resulting RPMS directory as a 3‑day artifact; a shared after_script locates the build directory, sets LD_LIBRARY_PATH for openSUSE jobs, and prints tshark version if built. Test jobs depend on their matching build artifacts, install the produced .deb or .rpm packages, execute tshark --version, and run with GIT_STRATEGY=none.
